name: Nectarine and Blueberry Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 2 sheets frozen puff pastry (17 1/4-ounce, thawed package)
2 pounds ripe nectarines, peeled and diced
1/3 cup firmly packed light brown sugar
Cinnamon syrup: 1 1/2 cups water, 2/3 cup sugar, 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, 2 tablespoons maple syrup, 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
Vanilla ice cream or frozen yogurt (to serve)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Lightly butter two 9x13-inch baking dishes.
3. On a lightly floured board, roll out each puff pastry sheet to form two 12x14-inch rectangles.
4. Spread one pastry with half of the nectarines, both kinds of berries, and nuts. Sprinkle with 2 1/2 tablespoons of brown sugar.
5. Starting on the longer side, roll the pastry up like a jellyroll and seal the edges with a little water. Cut into 2-inch slices. Repeat with the other pastry.
6. Place the slices, cut surface down, in the baking dishes, being careful not to fit them in tightly. Pour about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of cinnamon syrup over the dumplings in each dish.
7. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes. Serve warm with ice cream and additional cinnamon syrup, if desired. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
Cinnamon Syrup:
1. In a heavy saucepan, combine water, sugar, butter, maple syrup, and cinnamon. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
2. Lower heat and simmer for 8 to 10 minutes or until slightly thickened. Remove from heat.
